varieties.name: Glengarry
varieties.origin: East Kurrajong/Kyogle area, NSW, Australia, circa 1960s-1980s
varieties.traits: Small pale golden fruit with slight blush, vigorous tree growth
varieties.flesh_fruit: Very flavourful, great for fresh eating and cooking
varieties.growing_notes: One of the best options for subtropical humid subtropical climates. Specifically bred for low chill requirements. Performs well in humid conditions with proper fruit fly management. Fruiting in November-December.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery (Queensland), Heritage Fruit Trees Australia

varieties.name: Bentley
varieties.origin: Local selection from near Lismore, NSW, Australia
varieties.traits: Medium-sized fruit, hardy tree, similar to Glengarry
varieties.yield_notes: Produces good crops, comes into fruiting early
varieties.growing_notes: Well adapted to NSW subtropical conditions. Best suited to inland areas west of humid subtropical climates (Gatton, Toowoomba). Fruiting in November.
varieties.availability: Daley's Fruit Tree Nursery, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Low Chill (Carlo Berra Selection)
varieties.origin: Selected by the late Carlo Berra, Australia, 2000s-2010s
varieties.traits: Seed grown, medium size orange fruit
varieties.flesh_fruit: Brightly orange colored skin, sweet and juicy
varieties.yield_notes: Crops 2-4 years, crops abundantly in Kyogle
varieties.growing_notes: Specially selected for subtropical climates. Each tree slightly different but adapted to low chill.

varieties.name: Fireball
varieties.traits: Vibrant orange-red skin, medium size, grows to 4m
varieties.flesh_fruit: Sweet, juicy flesh with attractive color
varieties.growing_notes: Great for Queensland. Fruits late November to December. Performs well in humid subtropical climates area.

varieties.name: Gold Kist
varieties.traits: Medium to large with red-blushed yellow skin
varieties.growing_notes: One of the lowest chill varieties available. Good for canning, freezing, drying, and fresh eating.

varieties.name: Katy
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep yellow flesh, freestone, firm, mild and sweet
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for humid subtropical climates. Harvest in early December. Excellent quality fruit.

varieties.name: Royal Rosa
varieties.traits: Medium to large fruit with attractive rosy blush
varieties.growing_notes: Perfect for zones 9-10. Good for humid subtropical climates and coastal subtropical areas.

varieties.name: Divinity
varieties.traits: Slightly oblong fruits with red blush, 4m tall
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep orange, exceptionally sweet and juicy
varieties.growing_notes: Ripens late November to early December. Best suited to western humid subtropical climates suburbs. Self-fertile.
varieties.availability: PlantNet, Heritage Fruit Trees, Queensland nurseries

varieties.name: Bulida
varieties.flesh_fruit: Firm, very sweet and juicy, dark golden
varieties.growing_notes: Very well suited to inland areas west of Gatton. Fruits early to mid-December. Self-pollinating.
varieties.availability: Daley's, PlantNet, Garden Express, Ladybird Nursery

varieties.name: Newcastle Early
varieties.traits: Very early, medium pale orange fruits
varieties.flesh_fruit: Soft, sweet, loose stones, rich golden yellow
varieties.growing_notes: Old favorite from 1881 with low chilling. Very early season. Self-fertile.
varieties.availability: Lakeside Plants, Nursery Warehouse, Speciality Trees

varieties.name: Moorpark (Moor Park)
varieties.traits: Large fruit (5-7cm), deep golden orange with red blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Deep yellow, firm, exceptionally juicy with rich flavor
varieties.growing_notes: THE best tasting apricot historically. Favorite of Thomas Jefferson. Ripens late December-January. Not suitable for humid subtropical climates proper.
varieties.availability: The Diggers Club, Home Depot, Nature Hills

varieties.name: Trevatt
varieties.traits: Large round fruit, pale apricot skin with red tinge
varieties.flesh_fruit: Rich orange, firm, juicy, good sweet flavor, aromatic
varieties.growing_notes: Popular Aussie heirloom from 1900. Excellent eating, bottling, preserving. Ripens December before Moorpark. Self-pollinating.
varieties.availability: The Diggers Club, Heritage Fruit Trees, PlantNet

varieties.name: Tilton
varieties.traits: Heart-shaped, large, golden-yellow with rosy blush
varieties.flesh_fruit: Vivid orange, firm yet juicy, sweet with fine texture

varieties.name: Chulli (Wild Apricot)
varieties.origin: Indigenous to Himachal Pradesh (Kinnaur), Jammu & Kashmir
varieties.growing_notes: Traditional wild fruit. Used for chalori (sun-dried), chutney, jam. Kernels for oil. Cultural significance.
varieties.availability: Wild in Himachal - NOT available in tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Khurmani
varieties.growing_notes: Dried and sold as delicacy. Major economic crop for Ladakh farmers.
varieties.availability: Ladakh only - NOT suitable for tropical monsoon climates

varieties.name: Dovyalis hebecarpa - Tropical Apricot (Ceylon Gooseberry)
varieties.origin: Native to Sri Lanka and southern India
varieties.traits: Large shrub over 25 feet, deep green leaves, often thorny
varieties.growing_notes: THIS IS SUITABLE FOR tropical monsoon climates. Not to be confused with Mamey. True tropical species that thrives in hot, humid conditions. Available from tropical monsoon climates nurseries.
varieties.availability: iNetFarms tropical monsoon climates, Greens of tropical monsoon climates, tropical monsoon climates nurseries
